summary: The reproduction pushes a symlink into an attacker-controlled repository, then uses the web upload flow with a crafted multipart filename so Gogs writes the uploaded bytes through the symlink parent and creates a file outside the repository root.
expected_security_impact: Uploading a file into a repository should not create files outside the repository root, but the vulnerable build writes attacker-controlled bytes to an external path when the uploaded filename traverses through an in-repo symlink parent.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the pinned target, created an attacker repository, pushed a symlink named link that points at an external directory, uploaded a file named link\\owned.txt, and verified that the file outside/owned.txt was created outside the repository root with the attacker-controlled proof bytes.
verification_method: The helper script checks for the exact proof file under an external directory outside the repository root after the upload workflow completes. The run succeeds only if that out-of-repository file exists and contains the attacker-controlled payload.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed UPLOAD_WRITE_CONFIRMED after verifying the out-of-repository proof file.
The external proof file contained the exact payload string EVOHUNT-UPLOAD-PROOF.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
